transcript.content: Shape the refund auto-approval and privacy audit export rollout. I want to answer blockers in pieces; give me the shortest grouped questions needed before implementation.

Implementation is blocked because both surfaces still have execution-critical policy gaps.

Record-backed refund context: source exposes amountCents, riskTier, manualReviewRequired, provider idempotency, and failureReason; active records say those are signals, not policy authority.

Record-backed audit context: source exposes accountId, createdAt, status, balanceCents, email, and closedAt; no active record defines export fields, retention, closed-account inclusion, redaction, or owner.

Current grouped blockers:

1. Refund auto-approval: maximum amount, low-risk predicate, retry count/cadence, notification destination, failure/escalation behavior, and operational owner.
2. Audit export: exported fields, retention period, closed-account inclusion, PII redaction rule, and operational owner.

Answer in batches if needed. I will preserve concrete ratified values, keep unresolved semantic branches blocked, and avoid source/test edits or executable implementation tickets until each domain is independently ready.
